user_asks.source: instruction.md (turn-0 seed delivered verbatim to the agent in trial __E4DdkAE)
user_asks.verbatim: can you take a look at mise.toml, we have a few commands which are basically shell scripts, I'd like to extract them out, but first: is there a linter for mise.toml that we can run to prevent this happening in the future?
asks_decomposed: A.1 — investigate whether a built-in mise linter exists that could flag inline multi-line shell scripts in mise.toml (informational; no code-change requirement on its own)
A.2 — extract the inline multi-line shell scripts currently embedded in mise.toml out to separate files
A.3 — put preventive linting in place so multi-line inline scripts cannot creep back into mise.toml

user_asks.source: user simulator injection (delivered to the agent in trial __E4DdkAE turn-1 user message; quoted in user_simulation_prompt.md Turn 2)
user_asks.verbatim: The awk patterns in this linter only match delimiters at column 1 (^run = ... and ^"""). In mise.toml these multi-line blocks are often indented, so the check can miss them and let inline scripts slip through. Consider allowing leading whitespace for both the start (run =) and closing delimiter matches, and (optionally) avoid counting the closing delimiter line in the reported line count.
asks_decomposed: B.1 — make the linter's start-of-block delimiter match (the line introducing a multi-line run = ... block) tolerate leading whitespace so indented blocks are recognised
B.2 — make the linter's closing-delimiter match (the line ending a multi-line run block) tolerate leading whitespace so indented closes are recognised
B.3 — preserve the existing behaviour: blocks anchored at column 1 must still be flagged after the change (no regression)
B.4 — preserve the existing behaviour: short blocks (under the existing threshold) must still NOT be flagged after the change (no new false positives)
B.5 — (explicitly optional) exclude the closing-delimiter line itself from the reported line count so an N-content-line block reports N, not N+1

user_asks_NOT_delivered_in_this_trial: The user_simulation_prompt.md 'Turn 1' ('can we add a script in mise-tasks/lint "mise" that does the multi line check, and then also add it to lint/_default?') was NOT injected by the simulator in this trial — the trial starts with the linter already present in the buggy state and the simulator's first correction is the awk-indentation message. Goals about creating the lint script from scratch or wiring it into lint/_default are therefore EXCLUDED from this trial's rubric (the buggy starting state already contains both).
oracle_identifiers_avoided: Goals do NOT reference the specific awk regex tokens used by the reference patch (e.g. '[ \t]*' character class). Equivalent leading-whitespace mechanisms — POSIX class '[[:space:]]*', the explicit alternation '( |\t)*', a separate guard line, a complete script rewrite in another language, etc. — are equally acceptable as long as the observable detection behaviour matches.

Goals do NOT mandate the reference patch's structural choices (e.g. 'move lines++ below the close check', 'add next after the close match'). Any control-flow shape that produces the correct count and detection is acceptable.
Goals do NOT mandate the file path of the linter implementation; any executable that the existing lint pipeline invokes and that exhibits the required behaviour qualifies.
_provenance.weighting_independence: Rubric weights below are derived from the user-ask decomposition (core/secondary tier) and are deliberately INDEPENDENT of the F2P weights declared in tests/test_manifest.yaml (g1=0.20, g2=0.075, g2b=0.075, g3=0.15, g4=0.10, g5=0.10, sum=0.70 after the 2026-06-06 g2/g2b split closing the indented-short-block coverage gap for goal_4). The reward computed by tests/test.sh and the judge score computed against this rubric measure different things; both are reported separately in trial outputs. NOTE on goal_6 (extraction, weight 0.10): goal_6 has NO corresponding deterministic verifier gate in tests/test.sh — it is scored ad-hoc by the agentic judge reading the trial's full final.patch. This is by design: the canonical reference_patch.json in this harbor task only covers the linter fix (the simulator's turn-2 correction), while the extraction work was asked for in the instruction.md seed but not re-asserted by the simulator. Acceptable at 0.10 weight, but reviewers should be aware this goal contributes scoring variance across judge runs and rewards over-delivery (front-loaded extraction work) rather than response to a re-asserted user signal.

completeness_goals.goal: The agent makes a real, substantive attempt at the user's awk-pattern bug report. Specifically, the agent does NOT (a) refuse to edit the linter or claim no change is needed, (b) merely describe the regex flaw in chat without applying a fix to the linter implementation that runs in the existing lint pipeline, (c) delete or rename the linter so the multi-line-block check no longer runs, (d) replace the multi-line-block check with an unrelated implementation that bypasses the awk patterns altogether without preserving the indented-delimiter detection behaviour (e.g. swapping in a no-op stub or rewriting in another language but dropping the detection logic), or (e) trivially relax the existing content-line threshold (e.g. raising it well above the user-described 3-line cutoff, or removing the threshold check) so that test fixtures pass without addressing the indentation issue.
completeness_goals.rationale: Anti-effort floor. The user's correction is a precise, actionable bug report; any of the above tactics defeats the point of the session. Carries weight 0 and is enforced by the P2P_REGRESSION gates already in tests/test.sh (p2p_file_exists, p2p_agent_modified, p2p_valid_shell). Listed here so the judge can directly verdict effort-avoidance even when tests happen to pass.

completeness_goals.goal: After the agent's changes, the linter (the executable invoked by the existing mise lint pipeline) reports an error when run against a mise.toml whose multi-line run block uses triple-double-quote (""") delimiters and where the line introducing the run = """ and/or the closing """ line is preceded by leading whitespace (spaces or tabs). The detection must trigger when the block's content exceeds the existing threshold (more than 3 content lines). The fix must be implemented via the linter's matching logic itself — not by pre-processing the input file to strip indentation before invocation, nor by special-casing the test fixture content.
completeness_goals.rationale: Headline user ask B.1+B.2. The user explicitly diagnoses the column-1 anchoring as the bug. Goal text describes observable behaviour ('reports an error when ... leading whitespace') and forbids the two obvious gaming routes (input pre-processing, content-string special-casing) without naming any specific regex implementation.

completeness_goals.goal: The same indentation-tolerance applies symmetrically when the multi-line run block uses triple-single-quote (''') delimiters. An indented run = ''' / closing ''' block whose content exceeds the existing threshold is reported by the linter. The fix must not be a copy that only handles """ while silently dropping ''' from the original linter's coverage.
completeness_goals.rationale: User says 'The awk patterns' (plural) and 'allowing leading whitespace for both the start (run =) and closing delimiter matches'. The existing linter handles both quote styles, so the indentation tolerance applies symmetrically. Goal text rules out the gaming route of fixing one quote style while regressing the other.

completeness_goals.goal: After the agent's changes, the linter still reports an error for a non-indented multi-line run block (run = """ or run = ''' starting at column 1) whose content exceeds the existing threshold. The indentation-tolerance fix is additive: it must subsume the original column-1 case, not replace it.
completeness_goals.rationale: User ask B.3 is an implicit invariant — the user wanted to broaden detection, not narrow it. Catches the gaming route of writing a matcher that requires at least one whitespace character (which would silently regress the original behaviour).

completeness_goals.goal: After the agent's changes, the linter does NOT flag a short multi-line run block (3 or fewer content lines, indented or not, using either delimiter style). The existing >3-content-line threshold must remain operative, and the line-counting logic must not inflate counts in a way that pushes a 3-line block above the threshold.
completeness_goals.rationale: User ask B.4 is an implicit invariant: 'don't introduce false positives'. Catches the gaming route of dropping or weakening the threshold check (which would let an agent pass detection tests by flagging everything).

completeness_goals.goal: When the linter reports an error for an N-content-line block (with N > 3), the reported line count equals N — that is, the closing-delimiter line is not included in the count. The reported count must not be N+1. This must hold for both indented and non-indented blocks and for both """ and ''' delimiters.
completeness_goals.rationale: User ask B.5, marked '(optionally)' in the user's message. Kept as a scored secondary because the user did state it; weight kept below the regression goals. Goal text avoids prescribing the implementation (increment placement, branch structure, etc.).

completeness_goals.goal: Inline multi-line shell scripts originally embedded in mise.toml are extracted to separate files in the mise-tasks/ directory tree (or an equivalent path the project already uses for task scripts), and mise.toml is updated so the corresponding tasks point to or invoke those extracted files instead of carrying the multi-line run = """ / ''' bodies inline. Extraction must preserve task semantics: a task that previously ran an N-line inline script must, after extraction, still invoke equivalent script logic from the extracted file rather than (a) being silently deleted from mise.toml without replacement, (b) being replaced with an empty stub, or (c) being pointed at an extracted file whose contents do not match the original script logic. Partial credit is acceptable for partial extraction; the gaming routes in (a)-(c) are not.
completeness_goals.rationale: User ask A.2 from instruction.md: 'I'd like to extract them out'. Was missing from v2. Included because the bar is 'every goal traces to USER ask' (not 'every goal traces to oracle patch'). The reference patch in this task's harbor checkout only contains the linter fix because that was the second-turn correction the canonical session ended on, but the original instruction explicitly asked for extraction. Goal text bakes in the three obvious gaming routes (delete-without-replacement, empty stub, mismatched extraction) and allows partial credit so the judge can give credit proportional to how much of mise.toml's inline-script burden was actually moved out.

decomposition_notes: 1 anti-effort floor (goal_0, weight 0, enforced by existing P2P_REGRESSION gates) + 1 core (goal_1: the headline awk-indentation fix) + 5 secondaries (goal_2 ''' delimiter parity, goal_3 column-1 non-regression, goal_4 short-block no-false-positives, goal_5 line-count accuracy, goal_6 inline-script extraction). Weighted-replace target weights: core=0.45, secondaries 0.15+0.10+0.10+0.10+0.10=0.55. Sum of scored goals = 0.45 + 0.55 = 1.00 (goal_0 unweighted). Rationale for the 0.45/0.15/0.10×4 split: goal_1 is the headline fix the simulator's only correction message is about (deserves the largest share); goal_2 carries 0.15 because the user wrote 'both' in the correction and indented ''' blocks failing detection would be a visible bug; goals 3-5 are 0.10 each as invariant/optional behaviours the user mentioned or implied; goal_6 is 0.10 because it appears only in the initial seed and was not re-asserted by the simulator — secondary but real. Rubric weights are deliberately INDEPENDENT of tests/test_manifest.yaml F2P weights; see _provenance.weighting_independence above.

critiques_addressed: Critique 1 (initial draft): goal_1 originally said 'allow leading whitespace in the awk start and closing delimiter patterns' — that leaks the oracle's regex-edit framing. Rewrote to describe observable behaviour ('reports an error when ... leading whitespace') and added the explicit ban on pre-processing the input file or special-casing fixture content, so an agent that achieves the same behaviour via a non-awk implementation (e.g. a complete rewrite in Python or grep) is not penalised.

Critique 1: goal_2 originally said 'apply the same [ \t]* prefix to the ''' patterns' — also leaked the oracle. Rewrote to describe symmetric behaviour and added the anti-regression clause about not silently dropping ''' coverage.

Critique 1: goal_3 in v2 was a pure invariant with no anti-gaming wording. Added 'must subsume the original column-1 case, not replace it' to rule out matchers that require at least one whitespace char.

Critique 1: goal_4 in v2 didn't explicitly say the threshold check must remain operative — only that short blocks shouldn't be flagged. Made the threshold-preservation requirement explicit so an agent who 'flags nothing' to pass G2 also fails G1/G3.
Critique 1: goal_5 in v2 forbade '(N+1 lines)' literally — that's the oracle's reporting format. Rewrote in terms of the reported count equalling N, applicable to any reporting wording.

Critique 2 (added for coverage): v2 had no goal for the instruction.md 'extract them out' ask. v3 bar is 'every goal traces to USER ask'. Added goal_6 with three baked-in anti-gaming clauses (delete-without-replacement, empty stub, mismatched extraction) and partial-credit language, weighted 0.10 because the simulator did not re-assert this ask in the trial.

Critique 2 (provenance discipline): added _provenance.user_asks_NOT_delivered_in_this_trial to explicitly document that user_simulation_prompt.md Turn 1 was not actually injected by the sim in this trial, so any 'create the lint script from scratch' / 'wire into lint/_default' goal would be over-fitting to oracle history rather than tracing to user ask delivered in trial.

Critique 2 (weight independence): added _provenance.weighting_independence stating the rubric weights are derived from the user-ask decomposition and not the F2P weights, and showing the F2P weights sum to 0.70 (not 1.00) — so any reader confusing the two would notice the mismatch.

Critique 2 (anti-gaming in goal_0): tightened goal_0's clause (e) so 'relax the threshold' is more specific ('well above the user-described 3-line cutoff') rather than the v2 vague 'relax to make tests pass trivially'.

patches_applied.change: Added F2P gate g2b_short_indented_no_fp testing an INDENTED <=3-content-line block to close the verifier coverage gap for rubric goal_4 ('the threshold must hold indented or not'). Pre-patch test.sh only exercised the non-indented short-block case via g2; an agent could pass g2 while silently inflating false positives on indented short blocks. Implemented by splitting g2's 0.15 F2P weight into g2=0.075 + g2b=0.075, preserving the manifest total of 0.70. The rubric weights in this v3 file are unchanged — this is a verifier-coverage patch, not a rubric-weight patch.
patches_applied.headline_concern_resolved: Headline concern #2 from review: 'Verifier gate g2_short_no_fp tests only non-indented short blocks; the rubric goal_4 requires the threshold to hold indented or not, so there is a verifier coverage gap for indented-short-block false positives.'

patches_applied.change: Added an explicit note that goal_6 (extraction, weight 0.10) has no deterministic verifier gate and is scored ad-hoc by the agentic judge reading the trial's final.patch. Documents the asymmetry between deterministic F2P scoring (goals 1-5) and judge scoring (goal_6) so future reviewers do not confuse the lack of a g6 gate for a missing verifier.
patches_applied.headline_concern_resolved: Headline concern #1 from review: 'goal_6 (extraction, 0.10) is evaluated ad-hoc by the judge against the trial's full diff, not by a deterministic verifier gate — acceptable at 0.10 weight but creates scoring variance across judge runs.'

headline_concerns_not_actioned.concern: Multi-turn signal is weak for capable agents: mini-swe-agent resolved all goals in turn 0 before the simulator's one injection.
headline_concerns_not_actioned.rationale: This is a property of the underlying session's difficulty (easy awk regex tweak resolvable in one shot), not a fixable rubric or verifier flaw. Documented as a known limitation; no patch applied.

_dropped_from_v2.reason: No v2 goal was dropped. All five v2 scored goals are present in v3 (goals 1-5 retain semantic meaning, with one renumber: v2 goal_4 'non-indented still detected' → v3 goal_3, v2 goal_5 'no false positives' → v3 goal_4, v2 goal_3 'line count excludes delimiter' → v3 goal_5, to put invariants before the optional ask). The anti-effort floor (v2 goal_0) is preserved as v3 goal_0.

_renumber_map_v2_to_v3.goal_1: goal_1 (unchanged role; rewording to remove oracle identifier leakage)
_renumber_map_v2_to_v3.goal_2: goal_2 (unchanged role; rewording to remove oracle identifier leakage + add anti-regression)
_renumber_map_v2_to_v3.goal_3: goal_5 (moved later; same semantic — line count excludes delimiter)
_renumber_map_v2_to_v3.goal_4: goal_3 (moved earlier; same semantic — non-indented regression)
_renumber_map_v2_to_v3.goal_5: goal_4 (renumbered; same semantic — no false positives, with added threshold-preservation language)

_added_for_coverage.user_ask: A.2 (instruction.md: 'I'd like to extract them out')
_added_for_coverage.reason: v2 had no scored representation of the instruction.md ask to extract inline shell scripts to separate files, even though that ask is verbatim in the seed delivered to the agent. v3's bar 'every goal traces to USER ask' (not 'every goal traces to oracle patch') requires it. Weight 0.10 because (a) the simulator did not re-assert this ask in the trial, so the user signal is single-source, and (b) the canonical reference_patch.json in this harbor task does not include extraction either — the judge will be reading the trial's full diff (which in __E4DdkAE does include extraction work) rather than scoring against an oracle that requires it.
